# ID-33: Orchestrator-of-orchestrators polish — session-driver-cmux + Tier 2.2 hook (S62B fix-pack)

Address the three polish fix-Tasks surfaced by S62B orchestrator-of-orchestrators e2e verification — see `docs/research/orchestrator-of-orchestrators-e2e-findings.md` §3 for evidence and full per-fix change scope.

All three fixes are quality-of-life polish. None are correctness blockers; none gate Path A migration (per S62B Phase D §4: workflow-primitive verification PASS). They DO reduce friction when the o-of-o pattern is exercised at scale — recommended before next o-of-o dogfooding run (S62C planned).

## What's in scope

FX-1 — `stop-worker.sh` resilience polish (3 sub-issues bundled into one Subtask).
FX-2 — Tier 2.2 `git -C` hook subcommand whitelist (read-only ops should not be blocked).
FX-3 — `launch-worker.sh` collision diagnostic specificity (distinguish branch-collision vs worktree-path-collision).

## Acceptance criteria

- FX-1 (stop-worker.sh): three sub-issues fixed — untracked `.cmux-brief.md` no longer trips dirty-tree exit; cmux workspace close reordered to AFTER dirty-tree check (or partial-failure path explicitly documented); `--delete-branch` falls back to `git worktree list` lookup when meta missing.
- FX-2 (Tier 2.2 git-C hook): subcommand whitelist applied — read-only forms (status, log, show, diff, branch --list, worktree list, rev-parse, ls-files, ls-tree, remote -v, config --get, fetch) permitted from sub-agent context; mutating forms remain blocked.
- FX-3 (launch-worker.sh): pre-`git worktree add` checks emit precise diagnostic per collision shape — "branch already exists" vs "worktree path already exists".

Re-run of S62B Phase B Test #4 + #5 + #8 probes against the fixed scripts must PASS with no NEEDS-WORK on the three covered axes.

## Dependencies

None. Independent of all other Tasks. Safe to dispatch any time.

## Provenance

S62B Phase D finding pack — see `docs/research/orchestrator-of-orchestrators-e2e-findings.md` §3 (FX-1/FX-2/FX-3 detail) and the commit landing this Task.

## Subtasks

### ID-33.1: FX-1 — stop-worker.sh resilience polish (3 sub-issues)

- **Status:** done
- **Dependencies:** _none_
- **Updated:** _unset_

Three resilience improvements to `.claude/skills/session-driver-cmux/scripts/stop-worker.sh`. Source of truth: findings doc §3 FX-1.

**Test strategy:** Three unit-test scenarios: (1) launch + stop with untracked `.cmux-brief.md` only → clean exit 0 (no dirty-tree block); (2) launch + worker writes a real change + stop → block workspace close + exit 2 with NO partial cleanup (worktree + branch + events dir all still present); (3) launch + force-fail mid-stop + re-run with `--force --delete-branch` → branch correctly removed via `git worktree list` fallback. Verify each via direct invocation in a probe-worker dispatch (e.g. S62B-style `s62b-probe-fx1-*`).

**Details:**

TO BE POPULATED via Executor at {33.1} dispatch. Source of truth: `docs/research/orchestrator-of-orchestrators-e2e-findings.md` §3 FX-1 + §2 #4 side-findings 1/2/3.

Sub-issue 1 — Untracked `.cmux-brief.md` triggers dirty-tree exit-2 even though the brief is a script-managed artefact. Fix: exclude `.cmux-brief.md` from the dirty-tree check OR auto-delete the brief file as part of clean-shutdown.

Sub-issue 2 — cmux workspace is closed BEFORE the dirty-tree check runs. If the check fails (exit 2), the workspace is already gone but the worktree + branch are not removed. Partial-failure state with no recovery path other than manual intervention. Fix: reorder so dirty-tree check runs BEFORE workspace close — OR document that exit 2 is irreversible.

Sub-issue 3 — `--delete-branch` silently no-ops when the meta file is missing (post-failure re-run scenario). Fix: fall back to deriving the branch name from `git worktree list` when meta is absent.

<info added on 2026-05-21T20:35:00.000Z>
**S62C Wave 1 WP1 — FX-1 landed in two commits.**

Commit `cd9c9e9d` (initial WP1) — covers all 3 sub-issues at the script level:
  * Sub-issue 1: `.cmux-brief.md` carved out of dirty-tree check via `grep -Fvx` in the untracked-files component.
  * Sub-issue 2: reordered so dirty-tree check runs BEFORE `cmux close-workspace` — exit 2 now leaves workspace alive (operator can re-attach).
  * Sub-issue 3: WORKTREE_PATH + BRANCH_NAME fallback derivation when meta file absent (path convention + `git worktree list --porcelain` awk lookup).

Commit `7833411a` (follow-up) — WP4 verification surfaced a gap: the script-side carve-out alone wasn't enough because `git worktree remove` itself ALSO refuses dirty trees. Without the brief auto-delete, stop-worker exited 0 cleanly but worktree path persisted post-stop. Fix adds `rm -f ${WORKTREE_PATH}/.cmux-brief.md` immediately before `git worktree remove`.

Verification (per findings doc §6.2):
  * Sub-issue 1 PASS: probe s62c-fx-verify-2 (SID 3bbb244b) clean stop exit 0, worktree path removed, cmux workspace removed, branch retained.
  * Sub-issue 2 PASS: probe s62c-fx-verify-3 (SID 8adc23ad) injected `dirty-test-marker.txt` → exit 2 + workspace:24 STILL ALIVE post-exit (verbatim confirmed via `cmux list-workspaces`).
  * Sub-issue 3 PASS: --force --delete-branch cleanup of s62c-fx-verify-3 worked via meta-derived branch (meta-present canonical path); fallback path edge-noted in §6.2 (both meta + path gone = unresolvable; FX-3 collision tells operator branch name).

Files touched: `.claude/skills/session-driver-cmux/scripts/stop-worker.sh` (rewrite ~80 net lines), `.claude/skills/session-driver-cmux/SKILL.md` (extend `--delete-branch` doc + new Order-of-operations note).
</info added on 2026-05-21T20:35:00.000Z>

### ID-33.2: FX-2 — Tier 2.2 git-C hook subcommand whitelist

- **Status:** done
- **Dependencies:** _none_
- **Updated:** _unset_

Refine `.claude/settings.json` PreToolUse hook (`git -C` absolute-path block) to differentiate read-only vs write subcommands. Source of truth: findings doc §3 FX-2.

**Test strategy:** Re-run S62B Test #5 probe brief (`/tmp/claude-501/s62b-probe-b5.md` style) with the new hook — all 4 read-only abs-path commands (`status --short`, `worktree list`, `log --oneline -3`, `branch --list`) PASS; relative-path command continues to PASS; mutating abs-path command (`branch -D nonexistent`) BLOCKED. Parameterise into a fixture covering 10+ commands across read-only / write splits.

**Details:**

TO BE POPULATED via Executor at {33.2} dispatch. Source of truth: `docs/research/orchestrator-of-orchestrators-e2e-findings.md` §3 FX-2 + §2 #5.

Current regex: `git[[:space:]]+-C[[:space:]]+/Users/liamj/Documents/development/knowledge-hub` — op-agnostic, blocks all forms (read-only + write equally).

Design: two-phase check inside the hook command — (a) match absolute-path `git -C` call; (b) inspect the subcommand following the path. Whitelist (ALLOW) for read-only: `status`, `log`, `show`, `diff`, `branch --list`, `branch -v`, `worktree list`, `rev-parse`, `ls-files`, `ls-tree`, `remote -v`, `config --get`, `fetch`. Continue to BLOCK all mutating forms: `branch -d/-D`, `worktree add/remove/move`, `push`, `commit`, `merge`, `rebase`, `reset`, `checkout`, `switch`, `stash drop`, `clean`, `gc`, `prune`, `config --set`, etc.

<info added on 2026-05-21T20:35:00.000Z>
**S62C Wave 1 WP2 — FX-2 landed in commit `b6269317`.**

New external hook script `.claude/hooks/git-c-abs-path-subcommand-guard.sh` (~85 L). `.claude/settings.json` PreToolUse Bash[1] command now invokes `bash "$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/.claude/hooks/git-c-abs-path-subcommand-guard.sh"` — resolves via per-worktree toplevel so sub-agent worktrees inheriting branch state can locate it.

Two-phase design per FX-2 spec:
  * Phase 1: detect `git -C <abs-knowledge-hub-path>` shape; no-op if not matched.
  * Phase 2: extract subcommand; route via:
    * Unconditional ALLOW (16 verbs): status, log, show, diff, rev-parse, ls-files, ls-tree, fetch, tag, describe, reflog, blame, grep, shortlog, cat-file, symbolic-ref.
    * Conditional ALLOW for verbs with both read-only + mutating subforms: branch (ALLOW unless -d/-D/-m/-M/-c/-C/--delete/--move/--copy/--set-upstream-to/--unset-upstream); worktree (ALLOW only `list`); remote (BLOCK add/remove/rename/set-url/prune/update/set-head/set-branches); config (ALLOW only --get/--list/--get-all/--get-regexp/--get-urlmatch/-l); stash (ALLOW only list/show).
    * Default BLOCK for everything else.

Verification (per findings doc §6.1):
  * 30 isolated fixtures at authoring time: 13 read-only ALLOW + 14 mutating BLOCK + 3 non-matching ALLOW. All PASS.
  * Probe s62c-fx-verify-1 (SID c0c9fdab) end-to-end from sub-agent context: VERDICT PASS across 6 commands matching expected outcomes.

Files touched: `.claude/hooks/git-c-abs-path-subcommand-guard.sh` (NEW, +85 L), `.claude/settings.json` (replace inline op-agnostic regex with script invocation).
</info added on 2026-05-21T20:35:00.000Z>

### ID-33.3: FX-3 — launch-worker.sh collision diagnostic specificity

- **Status:** done
- **Dependencies:** _none_
- **Updated:** _unset_

Pre-`git worktree add` checks on `.claude/skills/session-driver-cmux/scripts/launch-worker.sh` to emit a precise collision diagnostic. Source of truth: findings doc §3 FX-3.

**Test strategy:** Re-run S62B Test #8 collision probe — verify each collision shape (branch-only / worktree-only / both) emits a precise diagnostic BEFORE `git worktree add` is called. Exit code 1 preserved. Verify via three direct invocations covering the three collision shapes.

**Details:**

TO BE POPULATED via Executor at {33.3} dispatch. Source of truth: `docs/research/orchestrator-of-orchestrators-e2e-findings.md` §3 FX-3 + §2 #8 LITE NEEDS-WORK.

Current failure mode: `Error: failed to create worktree at <path>` with exit 1 — operator must inspect to determine whether collision is on branch or worktree path.

Design: pre-`git worktree add` checks — (a) `git rev-parse --verify refs/heads/<branch>` → if exists, error `branch '<name>' already exists; previous worker stopped without --delete-branch? Recover with: git branch -D <name>`; (b) `[ -e <worktree-path> ]` → if exists, error `worktree path '<path>' already exists; recover with: git worktree remove --force <path>`; (c) otherwise, proceed with the original `git worktree add` flow.

<info added on 2026-05-21T20:35:00.000Z>
**S62C Wave 1 WP3 — FX-3 landed in commit `76d9eaeb`.**

Pre-`git worktree add` collision checks distinguish branch-collision vs worktree-path-collision before the opaque `failed to create worktree` error. Each shape emits a precise recovery hint. When both collide, emit BOTH diagnostics.

Design:
  * Reordered WORKTREE_PATH + BASE_SHA + BRANCH_NAME derivation above the collision check block (previously only WORKTREE_PATH derived early).
  * Branch existence: `git rev-parse --verify --quiet refs/heads/branch-name` → if exists, error: branch already exists + recover hint `git branch -D branch-name`.
  * Worktree path existence: `[ -e path ]` → if exists, error: worktree path already exists + recover hint `git worktree remove --force path`.
  * Exit 1 preserved (matches pre-FX-3 behaviour).

Verification (per findings doc §6.3):
  * Isolated logic test: shape 0 (clean) ALLOW; shape 1 (branch only) BRANCH error; shape 2 (path only) PATH error; shape 3 (both) BOTH errors. All PASS, cleanup verified.
  * End-to-end BRANCH-only collision via s62c-fx-verify-2 re-launch after non-delete stop: exit 1 with verbatim BRANCH diagnostic.
  * Order-of-checks note: pre-existing cmux-workspace-name collision (launch-worker.sh:140-145) fires first when cmux is alive, so FX-3 owns the post-cleanup recovery path (intended).

Files touched: `.claude/skills/session-driver-cmux/scripts/launch-worker.sh` (extend pre-flight section, +31 net lines).
</info added on 2026-05-21T20:35:00.000Z>
